differences.title: 1. REACTIVITY MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Uses fine-grained reactive primitives (signals) for efficient updates.
differences.rightDesc: Employs reactive stores for state management and propagation.

differences.title: 2. COMPILATION
differences.leftDesc: A small runtime is needed for reactive features.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles components into imperative vanilla JS code.

differences.title: 3. DOM UPDATES
differences.leftDesc: Directly manipulates DOM nodes with fine-grained updates.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles away the need for a Virtual DOM.

differences.title: 4. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: State changes are explicit via signal updates.
differences.rightDesc: Reactivity is often implicit within components.

differences.title: 5. SYNTAX
differences.leftDesc: Uses a JSX-like syntax for component structure.
differences.rightDesc: Utilizes HTML-like syntax with specific directives.

differences.title: 6. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Exceptional performance due to fine-grained reactivity and no VDOM overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Highly performant due to compilation and minimal boilerplate.

differences.title: 7. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Smaller but rapidly expanding ecosystem and community support.
differences.rightDesc: Well-established ecosystem with extensive tooling and community.
